overall.description: Game engine footage from a space simulation showing an orbital view of a planet with a thin blue atmospheric glow. The camera maintains a steady position above the planet's limb while two celestial bodies traverse the starfield, suggesting supercruise travel in deep space.

events.description: horizontal lens flare effect appears across the frame as the primary star aligns with the camera axis

agents.ego_description: piloting a spacecraft in supercruise mode, maintaining orbital position above the planet's atmosphere while observing the approaching star system

entered.detail: thin blue glowing atmospheric curve of the planet against the blackness of space
bright white stellar object providing primary illumination
smaller yellowish celestial body orbiting near the primary star
dense field of distant stars scattered across the black background

segments.scene_description: The scene presents a serene orbital view of a planet's curved horizon with a thin blue atmospheric glow. As the camera maintains steady position, the primary and secondary stars drift across the field of view from left to right. At approximately 12 seconds, a horizontal lens flare effect manifests as the primary star aligns with the camera, creating a cinematic lighting effect against the starfield backdrop.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot captures a serene orbital perspective of a planet that transforms into a cinematic display of light as celestial bodies drift across the frame. It begins with a steady view of the planet's curved horizon, highlighted by a thin, glowing blue atmosphere against the deep black of space. As the camera maintains its position, the primary and secondary stars gradually traverse the starfield from left to right. The scene then shifts in intensity as the primary star aligns perfectly with the camera's lens, triggering a bright horizontal lens flare that streaks across the view. This burst of light finally punctuates the stillness of the orbital vista, creating a dramatic contrast against the distant planetary limb.
